_comment: For Models A and B, E_{4He} = 400 MeV (100 MeV/nucleon, beta ~ 0.45).
Famiano injected particles at a SINGLE energy (monoenergetic injection),
not a power-law spectrum. The energy distribution evolves as particles slow.
Set spectrum.type = 'monoenergetic' for exact Famiano reproduction.
The mass injection rate M_dot [M_sun/yr] sets the absolute normalization.
Model A: M_dot = 0.1 M_sun/yr; Model B: M_dot = 1e-6 M_sun/yr.
For the non-thermal network, only the relative composition matters
— normalise to injected particles per unit time via n_dot_cm3_s.

composition_number_fractions._comment: Famiano simple jet composition, Table 3 / Section 2

_comment_energy: For 4He: E = 100 * 4 = 400 MeV (Famiano Table 3, Models A and B).
Famiano states jet energies are 50A-250A MeV, so 50-250 MeV/nucleon.
spectrum._comment_norm: Relative normalization; absolute scale set by mass_rate

mass_rate._comment: Used for model dynamics (cloud mass growth) — not yet coupled to cascade

_comment_A8: Famiano: 'only particles with A < 8 are treated as energetic projectiles;
heavier reaction products are assumed to thermalize immediately in the cloud'.
This constraint is applied in reactions.py via default_can_continue_nonthermal().

metadata.description: Famiano (2002) simple jet: p + 4He, monoenergetic 100 MeV/nucleon
metadata.reference: Famiano, Boyd & Kajino (2002) ApJ 576 89, Section 2 and Table 3
